---
title: Spatial analysis of glioblastoma
author: Siddharth Rajesh
---

## Loading the python libraries
```{python}
import scvi
import scanpy as sc
import squidpy as sq
import scrublet as scr
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import os

pd.options.future.infer_string = False  # add this after your imports

sc.settings.verbosity = 3
sc.set_figure_params(dpi=80, facecolor="white")
```

## Loading the scRNA-seq data

```{python}
# Load CellBender-corrected filtered matrices for 3 primary and 1 recurrent GBM samples
patient_1 = sc.read_10x_h5("results/cellbender/primary_tumor_001/primary_tumor_001_cellbender_filtered.h5")
patient_2 = sc.read_10x_h5("results/cellbender/primary_tumor_002/primary_tumor_002_cellbender_filtered.h5")
patient_3 = sc.read_10x_h5("results/cellbender/primary_tumor_003/primary_tumor_003_cellbender_filtered.h5")
recurrent_1 = sc.read_10x_h5("results/cellbender/recurrent_tumor_001/recurrent_tumor_001_cellbender_filtered.h5")
```

### Fixing the gene names and adding in the metadata

```{python}
for adata in [patient_1, patient_2, patient_3, recurrent_1]:
    adata.var_names_make_unique()   # Ensuring that the cell names remain unique

# Assigning the sample column and condition column for later integration

patient_1.obs["sample"] = "patient_001"
patient_2.obs["sample"] = "patient_002"
patient_3.obs["sample"] = "patient_003"
recurrent_1.obs["sample"] = "recurrent_001"

for a in [patient_1, patient_2, patient_3]:
    a.obs["condition"] = "primary"
recurrent_1.obs["condition"] = "recurrent"
```

## Run Scrublet to remove the doublets

```{python}
# Removing doublets that may have formed during the cell capture
# Scrublet simulates doublets and assigns a score per cell; predicted_doublet=True cells are removed downstream
for adata in [patient_1, patient_2, patient_3, recurrent_1]:
    scrub = scr.Scrublet(adata.X)
    adata.obs["doublet_score"], adata.obs['predicted_doublet'] = scrub.scrub_doublets()
```

## Concatenate all the samples into 1 AnnData object

```{python}
# Merge all 4 samples into a single AnnData; obs_names will be made unique to avoid collisions
ref = sc.concat([patient_1, patient_2, patient_3, recurrent_1])
```

#### Filter out the doublets

```{python}
ref = ref[~ref.obs["predicted_doublet"]].copy()
ref.obs_names_make_unique()     # Make the cell IDs unique
```

# Quality Control

```{python}
# Flag mitochondrial genes — high mt% indicates damaged/dying cells
ref.var['mt'] = ref.var_names.str.startswith("MT-")
sc.pp.calculate_qc_metrics(ref, qc_vars = ["mt"], inplace = True)
```

## Violin Plots

```{python}
# Visualize per-sample distributions of gene count, total UMI, and mt% before filtering
sc.pl.violin(ref, ['n_genes_by_counts', 'total_counts', 'pct_counts_mt'], groupby = 'sample', jitter = 0.5)
```

## Filtering out low quality cells and genes

```{python}
sc.pp.filter_cells(ref, min_genes=500)  # Filter out cells with < 500 genes
sc.pp.filter_genes(ref, min_cells=15)   # Filter out genes that are expressed in less than 15 cells
ref = ref[ref.obs.pct_counts_mt < 20].copy()    # Filter out cells that have mitochondrial content > 20%
```

#### Storing raw counts for cell2location

```{python}
# Cell2location requires raw integer counts — store before normalization
ref.layers["counts"] = ref.X.copy()
```

## Normalizing the counts matrix and scaling

```{python}
sc.pp.normalize_total(ref, target_sum=1e-4)
sc.pp.log1p(ref)
ref.raw = ref   # Save normalized for plotting

# Identify the 3000 most variable genes, computed per condition to reduce batch-driven HVG selection
sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(ref, n_top_genes = 3000, batch_key='sample')
sc.pp.pca(ref)         # PCA for initial dimensionality reduction
```

# Integrating samples using scVI

```{python}
# scVI learns a batch-corrected latent space across samples using a variational autoencoder
if (os.path.exists('h5ad_files/scvi_ref.h5ad')):
    ref = sc.read_h5ad('h5ad_files/scvi_ref.h5ad')
else:
    scvi.settings.seed = 42 # Seeding for reproducibility
    scvi.model.SCVI.setup_anndata(ref, layer = 'counts', batch_key = 'sample')      # Copy our annData to the scVI model
    vae = scvi.model.SCVI(ref, n_layers = 2, n_latent = 30) # Instantiate the VAE model
    vae.train()     # Train the model on the scRNA data
    ref.obsm['X_scVI'] = vae.get_latent_representations()   # Get the latent representation of cells used for clustering
    ref.write_h5ad('h5ad_files/scvi_ref.h5ad')
```


## Calculating neigbours and umap

```{python}
# Build neighbor graph in scVI latent space and compute UMAP for visualization
sc.pp.neighbors(ref, use_rep = "X_scVI")
sc.tl.umap(ref)
```

### Running Leiden clustering

```{python}
# Base leiden for all the clusters
sc.tl.leiden(ref, resolution = 0.5, key_added = 'leiden')

# Resplit cluster 6 at higher resolution to resolve sub-populations
sc.tl.leiden(ref, resolution=0.8, restrict_to = ("leiden", ['6']), key_added = 'leiden_resplit')

# Looking at the differences between normal leiden split and leiden resplit for cluster 6 at higher resolution
sc.pl.umap(ref, color = ['leiden', 'leiden_resplit'])

# Plotting the UMAP to see the clustering differences across samples and condition
sc.pl.umap(ref, color = ['leiden', 'condition', 'sample'])

# Checking the violin plot again to confirm QC passed integration
sc.pl.violin(ref, ['n_genes_by_counts', 'total_counts', 'pct_counts_mt'], groupby = 'sample', jitter = 0.5)
```

#### Checking the quality of clusters 12 and 14

```{python}
# Clusters 12 and 14 showed unusual QC profiles — inspect before annotation
sc.pl.violin(ref[ref.obs.leiden.isin(['12', '14'])], [
    "n_genes_by_counts", "total_counts", "pct_counts_mt"
])
```

# Annotation

## Dotplot of markers genes to annotate cells

```{python}
# Known GBM marker genes used to assign cell identity to each leiden cluster
gbm_markers = {
    "Tumor":            ["EGFR", "SOX2", "NES", "CDK4", "PDGFRA"],
    "Microglia":        ["TMEM119", "P2RY12", "CX3CR1", "HEXB", "SALL1"],
    "Macrophage":       ["CD68", "CD163", "MRC1", "CSF1R", "ITGAM"],
    "T_cell":           ["CD3D", "CD3E", "CD8A", "CD4"],
    "Treg":             ["FOXP3", "IL2RA", "CTLA4"],
    "NK_cell":          ["GNLY", "NKG7", "KLRD1"],
    "Oligodendrocyte":  ["MBP", "MOG", "PLP1", "OLIG1"],
    "OPC":              ["PDGFRA", "OLIG2", "SOX10"],
    "Astrocyte":        ["GFAP", "AQP4", "S100B", "ALDH1L1"],
    "Endothelial":      ["PECAM1", "VWF", "CDH5", "CLDN5"],
    "Pericyte":         ["PDGFRB", "RGS5", "ACTA2"]
}

sc.pl.dotplot(ref, gbm_markers, groupby='leiden', standard_scale = 'var')
```

### Identify the discriminating genes per cell type

```{python}
# UMAP overlaid with canonical marker genes to validate cluster assignments
sc.pl.umap(
    ref,
    color=["EGFR", "TMEM119", "CD3D", "MBP", "GFAP", "PECAM1", "leiden"],
    ncols = 3
)
```

## Cell-type map to annotate each cluster

### Assign cell types to each cluster

```{python}
# Manual annotation based on marker gene expression in the dotplot above
cell_type_map = {
    "0":  "Tumor", "1": "Tumor", "2": "Tumor",
    "3":  "Microglia", "4": "OPC", "5": "Tumor",
    "6":  "Tumor", "7":  "Unknown", "8": "Oligodendrocyte",
    "9":  "CD8_T_cell", "10": "Oligodendrocyte", "11": "Macrophage",
    "12": "Unknown", "13": "Tumor", "14": "Tumor",
    "15": "Unknown", "16": "Unknown", "17": "Endothelial",
    "18": "Tumor", "19": "Unknown"
}

ref.obs['cell_type'] = ref.obs['leiden'].map(cell_type_map).astype(str)
```

#### Sub-cluster for cluster 11 (Macrophage) for TAM subtypes

```{python}
# Cluster 11 is a mixed myeloid population — re-cluster at higher resolution to resolve TAM subtypes
sub = ref[ref.obs["leiden"] == '11'].copy()
sc.pp.neighbors(sub, use_rep = 'X_scVI')
sc.tl.leiden(sub, resolution = 0.3, key_added='leiden_sub')
sc.pl.dotplot(sub, [
    "CD68", "CD163", "TMEM119", "P2RY12", "CD14", "FCGR3A",
    "CX3CR1", "SALL1", "HEXB",   # homeostatic microglia
    "SPP1", "APOE", "CCL2"        # TAM-specific
], groupby = 'leiden_sub')

sub_map = {
    "0": "TAM_APOE",      # high APOE, CD163, SPP1 — immunosuppressive TAM
    "1": "Monocyte",      # high CD14, FCGR3A, CX3CR1 — monocyte infiltrating
    "2": "TAM_macrophage",# intermediate profile
    "3": "TAM_APOE",      # high APOE, SPP1, CD14
    "4": "TAM_SPP1",      # highest SPP1 — pro-tumorigenic TAM subtype
}

ref.obs.loc[ref.obs['leiden'] == "11", 'cell_type'] = sub.obs['leiden_sub'].map(sub_map).values

# Removing the unknown clusters
ref_clean = ref[~ref.obs['cell_type'].isin(["Unknown"])].copy()

# Plot the UMAP again with the annotated sub-clusters
sc.pl.umap(ref_clean, color='cell_type', legend_loc = 'on data')
```

##### Checkpoint the annData by saving it

```{python}
if os.path.exists('h5ad_files/annotated.h5ad'):
    ref_clean = sc.read_h5ad("h5ad_files/annotated.h5ad")
else:
    ref_clean.write_h5ad("h5ad_files/annotated.h5ad")
```

# Load the spatial data

```{python}
# Load 10x Visium CytAssist output — includes tissue image and spatial barcodes
spatial = sq.read.visium("results/spaceranger/GBM_CytAssist_outputs/outs")
spatial.var_names_make_unique() # Remove any duplicated gene IDs
```

## Quality Control for the spatial data

```{python}
# Calculate QC metrics for spatial spots — mt% flags necrotic/low-quality regions
spatial.var['mt'] = spatial.var_names.str.startswith('MT-')
sc.pp.calculate_qc_metrics(spatial, qc_vars=['mt'], inplace = True)
sq.pl.spatial_scatter(spatial, color='total_counts', library_id=list(spatial.uns["spatial"].keys())[0])
```

```{python}
# Filter low-quality spots: keep only in-tissue spots with sufficient gene coverage and low mt%
sp_qc = spatial.copy()
sp_qc = sp_qc[sp_qc.obs["in_tissue"] == 1].copy()
sp_qc = sp_qc[sp_qc.obs["n_genes_by_counts"] > 500].copy()
sp_qc = sp_qc[sp_qc.obs["pct_counts_mt"] < 25].copy()
print(f"Spots before: {spatial.n_obs}, after: {sp_qc.n_obs}")
```

###### Helper function for fixing arrow strings in annData object

```{python}
# Converts Arrow-backed string columns to Python objects — required for Cell2location compatibility
def fix_arrow_strings(adata):
        import pandas as pd
        def convert_df(df):
            for col in df.columns:
                if pd.api.types.is_string_dtype(df[col]) and not pd.api.types.is_object_dtype(df[col]):
                    df[col] = df[col].astype(object)
            df.index = df.index.astype(str)
            return df
        adata.obs = convert_df(adata.obs)
        adata.var = convert_df(adata.var)
        adata.raw = None
        return adata
```

#### Load the annotated scRNA-seq data and subset the data to genes in spatial data

```{python}
ref = sc.read_h5ad('h5ad_files/annotated.h5ad')

# Subset to shared genes between scRNA-seq data and spatial data
shared_genes = ref.var_names[ref.var_names.isin(sp_qc.var_names)]
```

## Run the RegressionModel in Cell2Location for estimating average mRNA expression signatures of cell types from single-cell/nucleus RNA-seq (scRNA-seq) data

```{python}
# RegressionModel learns cell-type-specific expression signatures from scRNA-seq
# accounting for batch (sample) effects — output is used as input to Cell2location
if os.path.exists("h5ad_files/ref_regression_c2l.h5ad"):
    ref_c2l = sc.read_h5ad("h5ad_files/ref_regression_c2l.h5ad")
else:
    from cell2location.models import RegressionModel

    ref_c2l = ref[:, shared_genes].copy()

    # Use raw counts
    ref_c2l.X = ref_c2l.layers['counts']

    RegressionModel.setup_anndata(ref_c2l, batch_key = 'sample', labels_key = 'cell_type')

    reg_model = RegressionModel(ref_c2l)
    reg_model.train(max_epochs=250)

    # Export posterior - no sample_df argument needed
    ref_c2l = reg_model.export_posterior(
        ref_c2l,
        sample_kwargs = {"num_samples": 1000, 'batch_size': 2500}
    )

    ref_c2l = fix_arrow_strings(ref_c2l)
    ref_c2l.write_h5ad("h5ad_files/ref_regression_c2l.h5ad")
```

#### Get cell type signatures and check that the model has worked

```{python}
# inf_aver is a gene x cell_type matrix of average expression — sanity check first 5 rows/cols
inf_aver = ref_c2l.varm["means_per_cluster_mu_fg"][
    [f"means_per_cluster_mu_fg_{ct}" for ct in ref_c2l.uns["mod"]["factor_names"]]
].copy()
inf_aver.columns = ref_c2l.uns["mod"]["factor_names"]

inf_aver.iloc[0:5, 0:5]  # sanity check — should show gene x cell_type expression values
```

# Run Cell2Location to integrate the scRNA-seq data to the spatial data

```{python}
# Cell2location deconvolves each Visium spot into fractional cell type abundances
# using the cell-type signatures from the RegressionModel above
from cell2location.models import Cell2location

if os.path.exists('h5ad_files/c2l_complete.h5ad'):
    sp = sc.read_h5ad("h5ad_files/c2l_complete.h5ad")
else:
    sp_full = sc.read_10X_h5("results/spaceranger/GBM_CytAssist_outputs/outs/filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5")
    sp_full.var_names_make_unique()

    # Split RNA and protein (antibody capture) modalities
    sp_rna = full[:, full.var["feature_types"] == "Gene Expression"].copy()
    sp_protein = full[:, full.var["feature_types"] == "Antibody Capture"].copy()

    # Transfer spatial coordinates
    sp_rna.obsm["spatial"] = spatial.obsm["spatial"]
    sp_protein.obsm["spatial"] = spatial.obsm["spatial"]
    sp_protein.uns["spatial"] = spatial.uns['spatial']

    # Cell2Location uses RNA alone
    sp = sp_rna[:, shared_genes].copy()
    sp.X = sp.X         # Raw counts required

    Cell2Location.setup_anndata(sp, batch_key = None)
    c2l_model = Cell2Location(
        sp,
        cell_state_df=inf_aver,
        N_cells_per_location = 10,   # Expected number of cells per Visium spot
        detection_alpha = 20
    )
    c2l_model.train(max_epochs = 30000, batch_size = 2048, train_size = 1, lr=5e-4)

    # q05 (5th percentile of posterior) used as a conservative estimate of cell abundance
    sp = c2l_model.export_posterior(
        sp, sample_kwargs={"num_samples": 1000, "batch_size": c2l_model.adata.n_obs}
    )

    sp.obs[sp.uns["mod"]["factor_names"]] = sp.obsm["q05_cell_abundance_w_sf"]

    sp = fix_arrow_strings(sp)
    sp_protein = fix_arrow_strings(sp_protein)

    sp.write_h5ad('h5ad_files/c2l_complete.h5ad')
    sp_protein.write_h5ad("h5ad_files/sp_protein.h5ad")
```

# Analysis

## Spatial scatter of all cell types

```{python}
# Visualize estimated abundance of every cell type across the tissue section
cell_types = sp.uns['mod']['factor_names']

sq.pl.spatial_scatter(
    sp,
    color=cell_types,
    ncols = 4,
    size = 1.5,
    img = False
)
```

## Spatial scatter of individual cell types of interest

```{python}
# Focus on the key immune and tumor populations for the GBM TME story
sq.pl.spatial_scatter(
    sp,
    color=["Tumor", "Microglia", "TAM_macrophage", "CD8_T_cell"],
    ncols = 2,
    size = 1.5
)
```

### Compute the spatial neighborhood graph

```{python}
# Grid-based graph with 6 neighbors — connects each spot to its direct Visium neighbors
sq.gr.spatial_neighbors(sp, coord_type='grid', n_neighs=6)
```

## Perform neighborhood enrichment

```{python}
# Check what's available in sp.obs
print(sp.obs.columns.tolist())

# Assign dominant cell type per spot based on highest Cell2location abundance
sp.obs["cell_type"] = sp.obs[cell_types].idxmax(axis=1).astype('category')

# Neighborhood enrichment tests whether cell types co-localize more than expected by chance
sq.gr.nhood_enrichment(sp, cluster_key='cell_type')
sq.pl.nhood_enrichment(sp, cluster_key='cell_type', figsize=(8, 8))
```

### Compute spatial co-occurrence probability

```{python}
import matplotlib as mpl
mpl.rcParams["text.usetex"] = False
mpl.rcParams["mathtext.default"] = "regular"

# Rename for plot compatibility — LaTeX underscore causes rendering errors
sp.obs["cell_type_plot"] = sp.obs["cell_type"].str.replace("CD8_T_cell", "CD8 T cell").astype("category")

# Co-occurrence measures how often two cell types appear within the same spatial distance bin
sq.gr.co_occurrence(sp, cluster_key = 'cell_type')
sq.pl.co_occurrence(
    sp,
    cluster_key = 'cell_type',
    clusters= ["Tumor", "TAM_macrophage", "CD8 T cell"]
)
```

## Perform Ligand-Receptor analysis

```{python}
# Permutation-based LR test (Squidpy/CellPhoneDB) — identifies significant interactions
# between spatially adjacent cell type pairs
sq.gr.ligrec(
    sp,
    n_perms = 1000,
    cluster_key = "cell_type_plot",
    copy=False,
    use_raw=False,
    transmitter_params={"categories": "ligand"},
    receiver_params={"categories": "receptor"}
)

# Filter to top 20 most significant interactions
means = sp.uns["cell_type_plot_ligrec"]['means']
pvalues = sp.uns["cell_type_plot_ligrec"]['pvalues']

# Focus on Tumor as sender; key TME receivers are TAM, CD8 T cell, Microglia
src = "Tumor"
targets = ["TAM_macrophage", "CD8 T cell", "Microglia"]

# Find top 20 L-R pairs by significance across all targets
top_pairs = (
    pd.concat([pvalues[(src, tgt)] for tgt in targets], axis=1)
    .min(axis=1)                        # most significant across targets
    .dropna()
    .sort_values()
    .head(20)
    .index
)

# Subset the uns dict to only top pairs for a cleaner plot
import copy
sp_sub = sp.copy()
for key in ["means", "pvalues", "metadata"]:
    for pair in list(sp_sub.uns["cell_type_plot_ligrec"][key].keys()):
        sp_sub.uns["cell_type_plot_ligrec"][key][pair] = \
            sp_sub.uns["cell_type_plot_ligrec"][key][pair].loc[
                sp_sub.uns["cell_type_plot_ligrec"][key][pair].index.isin(top_pairs)
            ]

ax = sq.pl.ligrec(
    sp_sub,
    cluster_key="cell_type_plot",
    source_groups=src,
    target_groups=targets,
    alpha=0.05,
    figsize=(10, 8),
)

# Fix legend overlapping axis labels
fig = plt.gcf()
fig.tight_layout(rect=[0, 0, 0.78, 1])  # reserve right margin for legend
```

## Compute Spatial Auto-correlation

```{python}
# Moran's I identifies genes whose expression is spatially structured (not randomly distributed)
sq.gr.spatial_autocorr(sp, mode='moran')

# Visualize the top 6 spatially variable genes on the tissue
top_svgs = sp.uns['moranI'].head(20).index.tolist()
sq.pl.spatial_scatter(sp, color=top_svgs[:11], ncols = 3, img =False)
```
